records.assistant_response_summary: Explained RF importance is still valuable as nonlinear sensitivity analysis independent of main model.

records.user_input: Questioned reliability of Pearson correlation
records.assistant_response_summary: Explained Pearson limitations and suggested Spearman/Kendall for nonlinear monotonic relationships.

records.user_input: Asked how to structure analysis for publication
records.assistant_response_summary: Proposed workflow: Poly2 → Spearman/Kendall → RF importance → SHAP → integrated discussion.

records.assistant_response_summary: Explained differences: global vs local, linear vs nonlinear, lack of interaction in correlation. Concluded SHAP reflects model-driven contributions.

records.user_input: Asked whether RF/SHAP use correlation data
records.assistant_response_summary: Clarified they do not use correlation; they rely on model structure and predictions.

records.assistant_response_summary: Confirmed it is acceptable; SHAP + importance is sufficient for nonlinear systems.

records.user_input: Asked relationship between dielectric constant, dipole moment, polarizability
records.assistant_response_summary: Explained physical relation: dielectric constant = dipole contribution + polarizability. Connected to ion solvation and conductivity.

records.user_input: Asked role of HOMO/LUMO in electrolyte
records.assistant_response_summary: Explained effects on redox stability, solvation, polarizability, and nonlinear influence on conductivity.

records.user_input: Requested full conversation as JSON record
records.assistant_response_summary: Generated structured JSON with translated and summarized dialogue.

feature_importance: Viscosity coefficient, HOMO/LUMO energies, and molecular weight are dominant descriptors
Dipole moment has moderate positive influence
Dielectric constant is less predictive as a bulk descriptor
modeling_insight: Poly2 captures nonlinear optimal content behavior
SHAP reveals feature interactions and nonlinear contributions
mechanistic_understanding: Ionic conductivity depends on solvation, viscosity, and electronic structure
methodological_guidance: Combine Poly2 + SHAP + RF importance for robust interpretation
Use Spearman/Kendall only as auxiliary trend indicators
